answer: In 2024, solar and wind surpassed hydropower in generation, and renewables provided 32% of global electricity. Renewable energy capacity grew by 15.1%, driven by solar, setting new milestones. By 2025, renewables are expected to overtake coal-fired electricity.

results.title: Record renewables rise in 2024 brings global electricity milestone
results.snippet: Solar electricity has doubled in just three years, providing more than 2000TWh of electricity in 2024. Wind generation also grew to 8.1% of

results.title: Solar Surge and Wind Wins: 2024's Renewable Energy Boom ...
results.snippet: Global renewables hit a record 582 GW in 2024. Solar and wind led the way as costs dropped, but barriers still threaten COP28 goals.

results.title: Renewable energy capacity surged around the world in 2024
results.snippet: Global renewable energy capacity grew by 15.1% in 2024, largely driven by solar. Yet a growth rate of at least 16.6% must be maintained to reach

results.title: Executive summary – Renewables 2024 – Analysis - IEA
results.snippet: Global renewable capacity is expected to grow by 2.7 times by 2030, surpassing countries' current ambitions by nearly 25%, but it still falls short of tripling.

results.title: Renewable energy hits 32% of global electricity in 2024, states ...
results.snippet: Renewable energy sources provided a record 32% of global electricity in 2024, according to a report from energy think tank Ember.
